task_yaml.task:
A friend sent me this cropped close-up of a painting with no caption and I'd
love to know exactly what it is and where the original lives so I can go see
it. The image is here:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/crop.jpg

It's only an interior detail — the title, signature, and frame are not in the
crop — so please identify the painting from the image itself, then trace it
back to the authoritative source.

Tell me:
- the painting's title and the artist,
- the approximate date,
- which museum holds it, and its catalog/accession record (the museum's own
  page or collection API, not a third-party reproduction),
- the museum object id / accession number if available.

Save your conclusion and the supporting evidence (how you identified it, and
the canonical museum record URL you traced it to) to
`/tmp_workspace/results/painting_provenance.json`, plus any notes/screenshots
under `/tmp_workspace/results/`.
